file_insights.key_content: Comprehensive CLI documentation with structured tables for environment variables and presets; includes examples for all major commands (generate, evaluate, auto-improve, presets list)

patterns_discovered.description: Environment variables and presets documented as markdown tables for quick reference and scannability
Documentation organized around command examples with full flags and options, enabling copy-paste workflow
README documents support for multiple LLM providers (Gemini, Chutes) as environment variables
Auto-improve command exposes iteration control and score targeting, suggesting sophisticated evaluation loop

gotchas_discovered.description: Documentation states 'at least one API key must be set' but doesn't explain fallback behavior or which provider is preferred if both are available
Generated file paths in examples use ISO8601 timestamp format (20240101T120000) but documentation doesn't explain how users should identify their own generated files
Presets table shows available presets but doesn't indicate where preset definitions are stored or how custom presets can be created
Example uses --iterations 5 --target-score 0.8 but no documentation on valid ranges or convergence guarantees

deliverables_met: Usage examples for all major commands included
CLI workflow structure verified (mention of full end-to-end verification in subtask)

recommendations.suggestion: Add explicit provider selection guidance and link to each provider's key setup instructions (Google Cloud Console, Chutes dashboard)
Add a minimal working example with only required parameters to reduce friction for first-time users
Document the structure and location of generated JSON files from generate/auto-improve commands
Add section explaining how to create custom presets or reference the preset definition file location
Add FAQ section covering common API key errors, network issues, and expected evaluation timeframes
